title: Escorted and functionalized guides for crispr-cas systems
abstract: The present invention generally relates to CRISPR systems or complexes, such as those with an escorted guide RNA.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A non-naturally occurring or engineered composition comprising an escorted single CRISPR-Cas9 guide RNA (esgRNA) comprising: an RNA guide sequence capable of hybridizing to a target sequence in a genomic locus of interest in a cell; and, an escort RNA aptamer sequence, wherein the escort RNA aptamer sequence comprises binding affinity for an aptamer ligand on or in the cell, or the escort RNA aptamer sequence is responsive to a localized aptamer effector on or in the cell, wherein the presence of the aptamer ligand or effector on or in the cell is spatially or temporally restricted. 2. The esgRNA of claim 1, wherein the escort RNA aptamer sequence changes conformation in response to an interaction with the aptamer ligand or effector in the cell. 3. The esgRNA of claim 1 or 2, wherein the escort RNA aptamer sequence has specific binding affinity for the aptamer ligand. 4. The esgRNA of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein aptamer ligand is localized in a location or compartment of the cell. 5. The esgRNA of claim 4, wherein the localized aptamer ligand is on or in a membrane of the cell. 6. The esgRNA of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein binding of the escort RNA aptamer sequence to the aptamer ligand, directs the esgRNA to a location of interest in the cell. 7. The esgRNA of claim 6, wherein the location of interest in the cell is the interior of the cell and the aptamer ligand comprises a cell surface aptamer ligand. 8. The esgRNA of claim 6, wherein the location of interest is spatially restricted within the cell. 9. The esgRNA of claim 8, wherein the spatially restricted location of interest comprises the cell nucleus or mitochondria. 10. The esgRNA of claim 8, wherein the target sequence is a Cas9 gene or RNA sequence. 11. The esgRNA of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the esgRNA further comprises an RNA aptamer linking sequence, operably linking the escort RNA sequence to the RNA guide sequence. 12. The esgRNA of any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein the esgRNA comprises one or more photolabile bond(s). 13. The esgRNA of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the escort RNA aptamer sequence is complementary to a target miRNA, and binding of the escort RNA aptamer sequence to the target miRNA results in cleavage of the esgRNA by an RNA-induced silencing complex (RISC) within the cell. 14. The esgRNA of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the escort RNA aptamer sequence is from 10 to 200 nucleotides in length. 15. The esgRNA of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the esgRNA comprises more than one escort RNA aptamer sequence. 16. The esgRNA of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the guide sequence is 10-30 nucleotides long. 17. The esgRNA of any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the guide sequence is operably linked to a tracr mate sequence. 18. The esgRNA of claim 17, wherein the tracr mate sequence is operably linked to the 3â end of the guide sequence. 19. The esgRNA of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the guide sequence comprises a seed sequence of 2 to 18 nucleotides, optionally positioned within 2, 3 or 4 nucleotides from the 3â end of the guide sequence. 20. The esgRNA of claim 19, wherein the seed sequence is at least 75%, at least 90%, or 100% complementary to the target sequence. 21. A non-naturally occurring or engineered CRISPR-Cas9 complex composition comprising the esgRNA of any one of claims 1-20, and a CRISPR Cas9 enzyme, wherein optionally the CRISPR enzyme comprises at least one mutation, such that the CRISPR enzyme has no more than 5% of the nuclease activity of a CRISPR enzyme not having the at least one mutation, and optionally comprising one or more nuclear localization sequences. 22. A non-naturally occurring or engineered composition comprising: the esgRNA of any one of claims 1-20, a CRISPR Cas9 enzyme comprising at least one or more nuclear localization sequences, wherein the CRISPR enzyme comprises at least one mutation, such that the CRISPR Cas9 enzyme comprises no more than 5% of the nuclease activity of the CRISPR Cas9 enzyme not having the at least one mutation. 23. The composition of claim 21 or 22, wherein the CRISPR Cas9 enzyme comprises a nuclease activity diminished by at least 97%, or 100% as compared with the CRISPR Cas9 enzyme not having the at least one mutation. 24. The composition of any one of claims 21-23, wherein the CRISPR Cas9 enzyme comprises two or more mutations of D10, E762, H840, N854, N863, or D986 according to SpCas9 protein or any corresponding ortholog, or at least one mutation wherein at least H840 is mutated. 25. The composition of claim 24 wherein the CRISPR Cas9 enzyme comprises two or more mutations comprising D10A, E762A, H840A, N854A, N863A or D986A according to SpCas9 protein or any corresponding ortholog, or at least one mutation comprising H840A. 26. The composition of any one of claims 21-23, wherein the CRISPR Cas9 enzyme comprises H840A, or D10A and H840A, or D10A and N863A, according to SpCas9 protein or any corresponding ortholog. 27. The composition of any one of claims 21-26, wherein the composition is in a cell. 28. The composition of claim 27, wherein the cell is a eukaryotic cell. 29. The composition of claim 28, wherein the eukaryotic cell is a mammalian cell. 30. The composition of claim 29, wherein the mammalian cell is a human cell. 31. The composition of any one of claims 21-30, wherein the composition comprises a CRISPR-Cas9 complex having at least three functional domains, at least one of which is associated with the CRISPR enzyme and at least two of which are associated with esgRNA. 32. A method for introducing a genomic locus event comprising the administration to a host or expression in a host in vivo of one or more of the compositions of claims 1-31. 33. The method according to claim 32, wherein the genomic locus event comprises gene activation, gene inhibition, or cleavage in the locus. 34. The method according to claims 32 or 33, wherein the host is a eukaryotic cell. 35. The method according to claim 34, wherein the host is a mammalian cell. 36. The method according to claims 32 or 33, wherein the host is a non-human eukaryote. 37. The method according to claim 36, wherein the non-human eukaryote is a non-human mammal. 38. The method according to claim 37, wherein the non-human mammal is a mouse. 39. A method of modifying a genomic locus of interest to change gene expression in a cell by introducing or expressing in the cell the composition of any one of claims 1-31. 40. The method according to any one of claims 32-39, comprising the delivery of the composition or nucleic acid molecule(s) coding therefor, wherein said nucleic acid molecule(s) are operatively linked to regulatory sequence(s) and expressed in vivo. 41. The method according to claim 40 wherein the expression in vivo is via a lentivirus, an adenovirus, or an AAV.
